title: print formatting of hex numbers, width does not match other simulators
body:
While formatting a hex print of a logic vector the width specifier is ignored.
My aim was to produce the same string width regardless of the logic vector width.
For a 32-bit vector the two formattings `%8h` and `%h` produce a string of the same width, 8 characters. 
For a 24-bit vector `%8h` should produce 8 characters and `%h` should produce 6 characters (24-bit/4-bit). 

I created a test on EDA playground to check how other simulators behave.
https://www.edaplayground.com/x/YevD

Verilator seems to ignore the string width specifier and always produces the a sting width aligned with the vector width.

I did check the standard, and I noticed it does not specify clearly what the extended bits should be.
Rivera Pro extends with spaces, while the big 3 extend with zeros regardless of the number sign.

I am using the latest Git main version of Verilator on Linux.
